title: Inclusive Interaction, Content, Responsive Design, and Human Evaluation · Product Reasoning Brief: Multimodal Meaning
summary: The primary flow works by mouse at one viewport but loses focus, status, meaning, readability, and error recovery for changed devices and users. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 multimodal meaning increment in accessible responsive product experience. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Provide text alternatives, captions, labels, non-color meaning, reduced motion, adequate targets, and tolerant input.
Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Multimodal Meaning while producing accessible responsive product experience.
